top.autoBlockTip: Auto-block drops any connection whose destination matches a threat-intel indicator — malware/C2 domains & IPs from abuse.ch feeds + built-in ({{domains}} domain / {{ips}} IP loaded). Heuristic detections (beaconing / DGA / large upload) only raise alerts; they never auto-drop.
top.guard: Mode guard: reverting to {{to}} in {{sec}}s — confirm you still have access.
top.tunNeedsRoot: TUN needs elevated privileges (start with sudo)

tunHelp.title: TUN mode needs elevated privileges
tunHelp.intro: TUN captures ALL traffic at the network layer, so it must create a virtual interface — that needs root / admin. It's also mutually exclusive with other TUN tools (Surge enhanced mode, other VPNs).
tunHelp.mac: macOS: start the whole gateway with sudo — sudo ./trust-proxy serve -c configs/config.tun.json  (setcap isn't available on macOS)
tunHelp.linuxSetcap: Linux: grant the binary the capability once, then run as a normal user — sudo setcap cap_net_admin,cap_net_bind_service=+ep ./trust-proxy
tunHelp.win: Windows: run the app as Administrator.
tunHelp.guard: Safe to try: a TUN switch arms a 60s auto-revert, so if it severs your access the gateway falls back to the previous mode on its own. Switching back to Manual never needs privileges.

top.routingGlobalTip: Global: default-deny OFF — unlisted traffic egresses via proxy
top.globalBanner: Global routing: whitelist default-deny is OFF — unlisted traffic egresses via proxy (blacklist / threat-intel / process·device gates still apply). Switch back to Rule to restore default-deny.

settings.languageDesc: Console display language. Defaults to your system language; a choice here is saved in this browser.

inbound.title: Proxy inbound auth
inbound.desc: Require a username + password on the local proxy inbound (SOCKS/HTTP at 127.0.0.1:17070). Leave both empty to keep it open (the default). Turn this on if other devices can reach the proxy port.
inbound.offHint: Inbound is open — no auth required.

tun.title: TUN options
tun.desc: Advanced tuning for TUN capture mode (full-device VPN that captures ALL traffic). Only applies when the capture mode is TUN; ignored in Manual/System.
tun.stackDesc: gvisor = userspace, safe & portable · system = faster, needs privileges · mixed.
tun.strictDesc: Force every packet through the TUN (recommended — prevents leaks).
tun.excludePh: one per line — routed AROUND the TUN
tun.excludeDesc: These apps bypass the TUN and go direct (Android package names).
tun.includePh: one per line — ONLY these go INTO the TUN
tun.includeDesc: If set, ONLY these apps are captured (mutually exclusive with Exclude).

acls.desc: Access control: what egress is allowed (whitelist) and what is explicitly denied (blacklist). Deny always wins over allow. No-Proxy picks the egress (direct vs proxy) — it never grants access.

whitelist.desc: Default-deny egress allow-list. Domains & IPs are destinations; Processes & Devices are opt-in source gates (empty = off).
whitelist.domainsHint: Suffix by default (example.com covers its subdomains). Wildcards: *.example.com (subdomains), foo* (prefix).
whitelist.processesHint: When non-empty, unknown binaries can't egress at all — even to a whitelisted destination.
whitelist.devicesHint: When non-empty, only these source IPs/CIDRs may egress. For gateway/router deployments.

blacklist.desc: Explicit egress deny-list. These destinations are REJECTED even if a whitelist or allow rule-set would otherwise permit them — the reject rules sit above every allow.
blacklist.domainsHint: Suffix by default (evil.com blocks its subdomains). Wildcards: *.evil.com, ads-* (prefix).
blacklist.keywordsHint: Matched as a substring anywhere in the domain.
blacklist.regexesHint: RE2 pattern matched against the domain (must compile).
blacklist.ipHint: Reject connections to these destination IPs/CIDRs.

directlist.desc: No-proxy (bypass) list: allowed traffic to these destinations egresses DIRECT instead of through the proxy. This is routing, not access — an entry must also be allowed (whitelisted or matched by an allow rule-set). Private/LAN ranges are always bypassed automatically.
directlist.domainsHint: Suffix by default (corp.local covers its subdomains). Wildcards: *.corp.local, intra-* (prefix).
directlist.ipHint: These destination IPs/CIDRs egress direct (never through the proxy).
directlist.builtinHint: Common private, loopback, link-local and CGNAT ranges are always routed direct and never proxied — no need to add them. Add your own domains/IPs below.
